title: CommonJS cron module will fail under package-wide ESM mode
reasoning: The package declares all .js files as ES modules, but the cron source is written as CommonJS. If Node loads src/cron/reminders.js in this package, require and module.exports are not available in ESM scope, so the reminder cron cannot start.
reproduction: Run Node against this file in the package, for example import('./src/cron/reminders.js') or node src/cron/reminders.js; Node treats it as ESM and fails before executing the cron logic.
recommendation: Either convert src/cron/reminders.js to ESM import/export syntax, rename it to .cjs, or move it under a package scope that declares CommonJS. Keep the importing call sites consistent with the chosen module format.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are provided for this feature, and package.json has no test script that would exercise Node loading of the cron module.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a smoke test that imports the cron module using the same module system used by the application startup and asserts startFollowUpReminders is exported.
minimumFixScope: src/cron/reminders.js and any application startup/import site that loads it

history.reasoning: The original evidence still exists unchanged in the root package scope: package.json line 5 still declares "type": "module", while src/cron/reminders.js lines 1-2 still use require(...) and line 122 still uses module.exports. The root startup path also still loads it with CommonJS from src/index.js line 10 and calls it at line 247, so this is not merely a missing or moved line. There is a separate server/src/cron/reminders.js copy loaded by server/src/index.js; that copy is under server/package.json, which has no "type": "module", so the server copy is CommonJS-scoped and not evidence of the ESM failure. Direct Node reproduction commands were attempted but rejected by the shell policy, so the conclusion is based on current package scope and module syntax evidence.
